text: If ever there was a perfect example of
Hollywood's ability to take a great
concept and slowly run it into the
ground with endless cash grab sequels,
it's got to be Jurassic Park. The
original movie was an all-time classic
of modern cinema, seamlessly blended
together, a compelling script full of
action, intrigue, and wonder. A great
cast of characters who actually made
smart decisions and acted like real
human beings. cuttingedge visuals and a
story that dealt with thought-provoking
ideas like the power and danger of
science gone wrong, man's attempts to
play God, and nature's ability to fight
back. It was one of the first movies
that I ever saw in the cinema and
probably the only film I went back to
rewatch multiple times. I say all this
because if you ever want to experience
the kind of cinematic whiplash that will
leave you paralyzed from the neck down,
then watch the original Jurassic Park
right before going to see Jurassic World
After Birth. A movie that I can best
describe as anti storytelling.
That is one big pile of [ __ ]
Driven by a paper thin and completely
nonsensical plot that takes forever to
get going and eventually peters out into
the most anticlimactic ending
imaginable. A bloated cast of characters
with no discernable personalities and
nothing even resembling brains or common
sense played by actors who seem to have
no idea why they're even in the movie.
It's really nothing but a series of
goofy, boring, and disappointingly
bloodless action scenes that mean
nothing, accomplish nothing, and made me
feel well, nothing. Let's get the plot
summary over with quickly so that
Universal Studios can hit me with yet
another frivolous copyright claim that
takes a month to resolve.

stars an affable looking Scarlett
Johansson as tough mercenary lady who
gets recruited by shady corporate guy to
lead an offthe-books mission to monster
dinosaur island, which is deep inside a
quarantine zone to recover genetic
material they can use to create a new
wonder drug to treat human heart
disease. Because dinosaurs apparently

don't suffer from heart disease.

 You know why? Because dinosaurs

staring at computer screens while
shoveling chemical laden crap down their
fleshy gullets. He's out of line. But

he's right.
 Anyway, I digress. To help
with the DNA extraction, they're
bringing along Science Man, who's an
expert in dinosaurs, but has never
actually seen one in the wilds. Oh,
yeah. And they charter a boat owned by
generic sidekick guy to get them ashore.
Things are going just great until they
get a distress call from diverse modern
family whose sailboat got attacked by
water. dinosaur. What the [ __ ] were they
doing inside the quarantine zone? How
did they even manage to get in there
without being spotted? What father in
his right mind would knowingly take his
children into such a dangerous place for
no apparent reason? Just don't think
about it too much. So, they rescue
Diverse Modern Family, but oh no. Then
they get attacked by the water
dinosaurs, which forces them to drive
their boat directly onto the rocks and
wreck it. [ __ ] it. Just go with it. And
for some reason, diverse modern family
all jump overboard and get separated
from the others for the rest of the
movie. But tough mercenary lady's not
about to give up yet and decides to
stick to the plan and recover the DNA,
then get themselves onto a rescue
chopper that will pick them up in 24
hours. Wait, if they have access to a
helicopter, why don't they just use that
to get to the island instead? Just don't
worry about it. But this isn't just any
old island, don't you know? It's Monster
Dinosaur Island where they left behind
all the genetic freaks that were too
scary or dangerous to use in the
original theme park. Wait, wouldn't that
mean that their DNA is all corrupted and
[ __ ] and too dangerous to use in humans?
Don't worry about it. Also, why are most
of the dinosaurs on the island perfectly
normal looking and would have made
excellent additions to the park? Don't
worry about it. So they get the DNA and
reunite with diverse modern family at an
abandoned research base which is somehow
still fully functional after 20 years.
Then the Ryan Canelosaurus shows up and
eats shady corporate guy and generic
sidekick guy sacrifices himself to let
the others escape. But then he turns out
to be okay anyway because he survived
somehow. How exactly did he manage to
get away from the Ryan Canelosaurus?
Just don't worry about it. Then they all
leave and that's it. That's the movie
over. Everyone in this room is now
dumber for having listened to it. I
award you no points and may God have

mercy on your soul.
 You know, my first
thought when the credits rolled was,
what the [ __ ] was the point in this
film? I mean, it's basically a bunch of
people I don't care about going to a
place I'm not particularly interested in
to do a thing that doesn't really matter
anyway. Then there's all the abandoned
plot threads that are introduced but go
absolutely nowhere like the ancient
ruins in the cliffside or the crash
chopper from a previous expedition or
the traumatic backstory for tough
mercenary lady. It's like the studio
came up with the plot by doing a single
brainstorming session, taking the first
pitch that someone spitballled together
and just ran with it. And before anyone
jumps in with the old it's a Jurassic
Park movie drinker, it's not supposed to
be smart argument. Fine. I can handle a
dumb thrill ride if that's what these
movies have devolved into now, but I at
least expect it to make some kind of
[ __ ] sense. And that's the thing,
this movie doesn't. The plot relies on
the most insane contrivances and
coincidences just to hold it together.
Like, oh my goodness, isn't it
convenient that diverse modern family
and tough mercenary girl just happen to
arrive at the same place at the same
time for the finale? or how literally
the entire movie is predicated on a
Snickers rapper getting sucked into a
[ __ ] air vent. I'm not kidding, by
the way. That's literally what kicks off
the entire events of this film.
Character motivations meander all over
the place with zero explanation. The
cast is way too bloated for the plot
that sustains them. And the narrative
jumps backwards and forwards between
diverse Modern Family and tough
mercenary ladies grouped with such
massive chunks that you actually start
to forget what everyone's doing and why.
And I'm not kidding. The diverse Modern
Family are such a bunch of hapless
comedy retards that I was actually
rooting for the dinosaurs by the ends.
The script also can't seem to decide who
the main character is. I mean, it should
probably be Science Man since she's a
more logical and relatable POV character
than Tough Mercenary Lady, but I guess
Scarlett Johansson's a bigger name, so
she's awkwardly shuffled into the top
spot instead. The problem, though, is
the script can't seem to decide what
kind of character she even is. Is she a
cold-hearted killer who's just out for
money, a quippy and light-hearted hero
with a flirtatious side, or a troubled
ex soldier with a dark past? I don't
[ __ ] know. The script tries to make
her a bit of everything, and it's just
as tonally diluted as you might think.
Scarlett Johansson struggles through the
movie with the faintly baffled smile of
a star who can't wait to cash that
paycheck and get the [ __ ] out of there.
And quite frankly, I don't blame her.
Generic sidekick guy is a perfect
example of how to waste a really good
actor. Mahersula Ali is a talent just
waiting to be used, but the movie gives
him nothing to do. He's just kind of
there, functionally identical to tough
mercenary lady with the same backstory
and personality and eating up a lot of
character beats that should have gone to
her insteads. You know, it's really not
that hard to come up with a fun story
for a Jurassic World movie, but holy
[ __ ] you'd never think so from watching
this steaming pile of dinosaur [ __ ]
Jurassic World Afterbirth is everything
I hate about modern cinema. It's what
happens when a studio has too much
money, too much resources, and
absolutely no idea what to do with any
of it. It's a movie that should have
been aborted long before it emerged into
the world. And if you value your wallet
or your sanity, you'll skip this one and
just rewatch the original instead.
Believe me, you'll have a lot more fun.
Anyway, that's all I've got for today.
Go away now.
